items.title: How the world failed Ryan and Yaman
items.summary: An Israeli strike killed my children. What pains me is not just their loss but also the normalisation of their murder.

items.title: Ukraine’s forcibly transferred children must not be a bargaining chip
items.summary: Russia must face more pressure to fulfil its legal obligations to return the Ukrainian children.

items.title: Residents flee Beirut’s southern suburbs as Israel orders strikes
items.summary: Videos show gridlocked traffic in the southern suburbs of Beirut as residents rushed to evacuate the Dahiyeh area.

items.title: How could El Nino reshape tropical storms around the world this year?
items.summary: El Nino tends to reduce hurricanes in the Atlantic while increasing storms in the Pacific Ocean.

items.title: Greece reopens Syrian and Afghan asylum cases, hoping for returns
items.summary: Greece's migration minister recently said Athens does not share common values with 'hardcore Islam'.

items.title: England World Cup 2026 team preview: Players to watch, group and squad list
items.summary: England cruised through qualifying, but some fans are concerned over the squad selection and the form against top teams.

items.summary: French Navy seizes Russian oil tanker

items.title: What is Lebanon’s Beaufort Castle, and why has Israel captured it?
items.summary: The fortress, a historical battleground, is playing a central role in Israel’s military campaign against Hezbollah.

items.title: Kuwait condemns Iranian attack as Iran-US trade new strikes
items.summary: Kuwait activated its air defences after what its foreign ministry later described as a ‘heinous Iranian attack’.

items.title: French navy, backed by the UK, intercepts Russian oil tanker
items.summary: According to France, the tanker, which had sailed from Murmansk, was trying to 'skirt international sanctions'.

items.title: Ethiopia holds elections with PM Abiy’s party expected to dominate
items.summary: Voting will not take place in northern Tigray region and some parts of the Amhara region amid insecurity.

items.title: Israel bombs a beloved Lebanese landmark, chef Husen Fayad’s restaurant
items.summary: Israel destroyed Arch Almoulouk, a resort and restaurant owned by famous Lebanese chef Husen Fayad in Wadi al-Hujeir.

items.summary: Washington and Tehran are attacking each other despite a ceasefire that came into effect on April 8.

items.summary: Ultra-Orthodox Jewish demonstrators storm Israeli police station

items.title: Which football rule changes will be implemented during the World Cup?
items.summary: Red cards for covering mouths, corner kicks for time wasting and broadened use of VAR technology will be implemented.

items.title: Iran’s IRGC launches retaliatory strike after US attacks
items.summary: Kuwait, which hosts US forces, says its defence systems intercepted missiles and drones amid sirens across the country.

items.title: Political commentators Cenk, Piker say UK bars them over Israel remarks
items.summary: The US citizens allege that they have been denied entry to Britain due to their outspoken criticism of Israel.

items.title: Vinicius brings Maracana curtain-raiser to life as Brazil rout Panama 6-2
items.summary: Christian Pulisic breaks goal drought as US beat Senegal 3-2 in international friendly ahead of World Cup.

items.title: Lebanese start fleeing south Beirut after Israel says will target the area
items.summary: The Israeli government says it has given the army orders to attack the southern suburbs of the capital.

items.summary: While a peace deal between the US and Iran remains elusive, Israel has deepened its offensive into southern Lebanon.

items.title: Tense election night in Colombia as country heads to presidential runoff
items.summary: Colombia’s election heads to a June 21 runoff after a tight first round between de la Espriella and Cepeda.

items.title: Israel airstrike kills at least two Palestinians at Gaza port
items.summary: At least two Palestinians were killed and around a dozen wounded when an Israeli air strike hit a crowded cafe.

items.title: Ex-US Fed Chair Powell warns against politicisation amid Trump’s attacks
items.summary: Jerome Powell says the US central bank is undergoing a 'stress test' like other institutions in the current era.

items.title: Sheinbaum accuses US of ‘interference’ amid probe of Mexican officials
items.summary: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum has accused the United States of interfering in Mexico’s internal affairs.

items.title: US says ban on AI chip shipments applies to Chinese firms outside China
items.summary: Department of Commerce issues guidance on chip restrictions amid concerns about loopholes in export control regime.
